Protocol.label: The methodology
Protocol.title: The Protocol
Protocol.sub: In high-stakes legal conflict, the outcome is determined before the first move is made. Our method is proprietary, disciplined, and relentless.

phase1.title: Phase 1: Diagnosis & Battlefield Mapping
phase1.desc: Deep research into the opponent’s position, incentives, and weaknesses. We don't just study the law; we study the context.

phase2.title: Phase 2: Strategy Construction
phase2.desc: Defining the 'Versus'—identifying the exact leverage points where the adversary is most vulnerable.

phase3.title: Phase 3: Relentless Execution
phase3.desc: Precise, uncompromising legal work. We maintain pressure until the objective is secured.

IntakeForm.label: Start here
IntakeForm.question: What are we fighting today?
IntakeForm.sub: No forms. No friction. A brief conversation to understand your situation — so when we speak, we're already aligned.
IntakeForm.confirmation: Vache will review your matter personally and reach out within 24 hours. Expect directness, not boilerplate.

items.title: Commercial Dispute Strategy: ₽47M Recovery
items.excerpt: Challenge: A complex supply chain dispute involving three jurisdictions. Strategy: Immediate asset freeze followed by surgical pressure on the opponent's main credit line. Resolution: Full recovery plus costs settled in 48 hours.

items.title: Corporate Restructuring: 90-Day Execution
items.excerpt: Challenge: A fragmented holding structure causing tax friction. Strategy: Cross-border consolidation through a tech-zone holding company. Resolution: 15% efficiency gain and complete regulatory clearance.

items.title: Doing Business in Armenia: 2024 Strategic Guide
items.excerpt: A high-level briefing for foreign investors on regulatory nuances, tax incentives (IT Free Zones), and common pitfalls.

doing-business-in-armenia.title: Gateway to Armenia: A Strategic Briefing
doing-business-in-armenia.intro: Armenia offers a unique intersection of high-skilled human capital and significant tax incentives. However, navigating the regulatory nuances requires a focus on structural integrity.

sections.title: Regulatory Nuances
sections.content: Unlike many CIS jurisdictions, Armenia has robust protections for foreign investors, including the right to own 100% of local subsidiaries and no restrictions on profit repatriation.

sections.title: Tax Incentives & IT Free Zones
sections.content: The IT sector enjoys a 0% corporate tax rate (until 2025/2026) and significantly reduced income tax (10%). Leveraging these zones requires precise corporate structuring from day one.

sections.title: Common Pitfalls
sections.content: The most common failure for international firms is assuming 'standard' templates will work. Local employment laws and real estate title chains require specific Armenian expertise.

Footer.tagline: Precision legal strategy for those who can't afford anything less.
Footer.newsletterSub: Receive high-level insights on the Armenian legal landscape.

commercial-litigation.title: Commercial Litigation
commercial-litigation.conflict: Shareholder disputes and breach of contract can paralyze a growing enterprise. In the Armenian market, uncertainty in enforcement often leads to multi-year stagnation.
commercial-litigation.versus: We don’t just litigate; we architect a settlement before the first hearing. Our approach is to define the conflict so precisely that the adversary’s position becomes untenable.

faq.q: How long does a commercial dispute take in Armenia?
faq.a: Direct litigation can take 12-24 months. Our strategy aims for resolution within 6 months through aggressive tactical pressure.
Yes, if applied with surgical precision to freeze assets before they are moved.

corporate-advisory.title: Corporate Advisory
corporate-advisory.conflict: Poor governance is a slow-acting poison. Founders often skip the 'rules of the game' in favor of speed, leading to terminal conflict later.
corporate-advisory.versus: We build corporate architectures that anticipate crisis. We structure for conflict so that when it arrives, your position is already fortified.

faq.q: What is the best jurisdiction for a holding company?
faq.a: It depends on your exit strategy. Armenia offers unique tax incentives for IT and Free Zones that we leverage.

real-estate-property.title: Real Estate & Property
real-estate-property.conflict: Property titles in emerging markets can be deceptively fragile. One historical error in a title chain can invalidate a multi-million dollar investment.
real-estate-property.versus: Exhaustive legal due diligence that goes beyond the certificate. We map the history of every title to eliminate surprises.

faq.q: Can foreigners own land in Armenia?
faq.a: No, but they can own buildings and have long-term leasehold rights for the land, which is practically identical in use-case.

contract-architecture.title: Contract Architecture
contract-architecture.conflict: Standardized templates are administrative friction, not legal protection. They fail exactly when you need them.
contract-architecture.versus: We draft agreements to withstand pressure. Every clause is a defensive line designed to ensure that if a breach occurs, the resolution is already written in your favor.

faq.q: Why not use a standard template?
faq.a: Because standard templates don't account for your specific leverage. A custom contract is an offensive weapon.

regulatory-compliance.title: Regulatory & Compliance
regulatory-compliance.conflict: Regulatory landscapes in Armenia change faster than businesses can adapt. Compliance is often mistaken for a checkbox, but it's a license to operate.
regulatory-compliance.versus: Proactive navigation. We don't just tell you what the law is; we tell you where it's going and how to stay ahead of the regulator.

faq.q: Is the Central Bank of Armenia startup-friendly?
faq.a: Yes, but they demand absolute transparency. We bridge that gap.

international-arbitration.title: International Arbitration
international-arbitration.conflict: State courts can be unpredictable for foreign entities. Arbitration is the gold standard for neutrality, but it is expensive and slow if mismanaged.
international-arbitration.versus: We treat arbitration as a strategic contest. Our goal is to win the proceedings by out-preparing the claimant before the first tribunal meeting.

faq.q: Is arbitration better than state courts in Armenia?
faq.a: For international contracts, absolutely. It ensures a level playing field and enforceable finality.
